---
title: "League Prediction"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

![](images/clipboard-133223893.png)

## ![](images/clipboard-4290094380.png)Binary Classification - League of Legends Win prediction at 10 minutes

#### Import Libraries

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(reticulate)
library(DescTools)
library(DataExplorer)
library(pROC)
library(ggstatsplot)
library(caret)
library(car)
library(glmnet)
library(ranger)
library(kernlab)
library(catboost)
library(ParBayesianOptimization)
library(lightgbm)
library(doParallel)
library(Amelia)
library(randomForest)
library(shapviz)
library(kernelshap)
library(ggplot2)
```

#### Import Data

```{r}
data <- read.csv('high_diamond_ranked_10min.csv')

#remove index
index <- data[,1]
data <- data[,-1]


#make the response variable a factor
data$blueWins <- as.factor(data$blueWins)
```

#### Perform Eda

```{r}
#examine for missing data
missmap(data) #we notice there is no missing data

#summary statistics with plots
Desc(data, plotit = T)

#distribution plots, split by outcome. Create a function so that we can use again later easily
distplot <- function(x) {
  data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(x), names_to = "cols", values_to = "values") %>%
  ggplot(aes(values, fill = factor(blueWins), color = factor(blueWins))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ cols, scales = "free") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "red", "1" = "blue"),
                    labels = c("0" = "Red Wins", "1" = "Blue Wins")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("0" = "red", "1" = "blue"),
                     labels = c("0" = "Red Wins", "1" = "Blue Wins")) +
  labs(fill = "Outcome", color = "Outcome") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

distplot(2:ncol(data))

#eda report 
create_report(data, y = "blueWins")

#correlation plot 
ggcorrmat(data, matrix.type = 'lower',method = 'nonparametric')
```

#### Removing Variables and Feature Engineering

```{r}
# we can start by removing redundant columns
# these three are just opposites of the blue counterpart
data$redFirstBlood <- NULL 
data$redGoldDiff <- NULL
data$redExperienceDiff <- NULL
data$redKills <- NULL
data$redDeaths <- NULL

#these four are just totals / 10 since each observation is at the 10 min mark
data$blueGoldPerMin <- NULL
data$redGoldPerMin <- NULL
data$blueCSPerMin <- NULL
data$redCSPerMin <- NULL


# now we create some features

data$goldshare <- data$blueTotalGold / (data$blueTotalGold + data$redTotalGold) #this is the % share of the gold in the game that blue team owns
data$xpshare <- data$blueTotalExperience / (data$blueTotalExperience + data$redTotalExperience) #this is the % share of the experience in the game that blue team owns

data$blueObjectives <- data$blueDragons + data$blueHeralds # combine the objective scores
data$redObjectives <- data$redDragons + data$redHeralds # same for red

#now check the distribution plots to see if useful
distplot((ncol(data)-3):ncol(data)) # we see that all four engineered features are useful for seperating the outcome
```

#### Train Validate Test Split

```{r}
set.seed(0318)

#split off the test set
index <- createDataPartition(data$blueWins, p = 0.9, list = FALSE)

test <- data[-index,]
train <- data[index,]

#now split rest into train and validate
index2 <- createDataPartition(train$blueWins, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

validate <- train[-index2,]
train <- train[index2,]

#check the size of each
nrow(train) / nrow(data)
nrow(validate) / nrow(data)
nrow(test) / nrow(data)

#split off dependant and independant columns
x_train <- train %>% select(-blueWins)
y_train <- train$blueWins
x_validate <- validate %>% select(-blueWins)
y_validate <- validate$blueWins
x_test <- test %>% select(-blueWins)
y_test <- test$blueWins

#now we scale, fitting on train and applying to validate and test to avoid leakage

#we will also remove near zero variance columns
pre_proc <- preProcess(x_train, method = c("center", "scale", "nzv"))

x_train <- predict(pre_proc, x_train)
train <- cbind(blueWins = y_train, x_train)
x_validate <- predict(pre_proc, x_validate)
validate <- cbind(blueWins = y_validate, x_validate)
x_test <- predict(pre_proc, x_test)
test <- cbind(blueWins = y_test, x_test)

#examine which columns were lost
setdiff(names(data), names(x_train)) #the towers destroyed columns were removed - makes sense as it is uncommon for towers to be destroyed at or before 10 minutes
```

#### Create an ROC-AUC Function to use

```{r}
roc_auc <- function(actual, probs, model){
  roc_obj <- roc(actual, probs)
  
  roc_df <- data.frame(
    fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities,
    tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities
  )
  best_t <- coords(roc_obj, "best", best.method = "youden")

  ggplot(roc_df, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_abline(lty = 2, color = "gray50") +
  
  # Youden's J vertical drop line
  annotate("segment",
           x = 1 - best_t$specificity, xend = 1 - best_t$specificity,
           y = 0, yend = best_t$sensitivity,
           color = "red", lty = 2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  # Youden's J horizontal line
  annotate("segment",
           x = 0, xend = 1 - best_t$specificity,
           y = best_t$sensitivity, yend = best_t$sensitivity,
           color = "red", lty = 2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  # Optimal point
  annotate("point",
           x = 1 - best_t$specificity, y = best_t$sensitivity,
           color = "red", size = 4) +
  
  # Label box
  annotate("label",
           x = 1 - best_t$specificity + 0.02,
           y = best_t$sensitivity - 0.1,
           label = paste0("Threshold: ", round(best_t$threshold, 3),
                          "\nSens: ",    round(best_t$sensitivity, 3),
                          "\nSpec: ",    round(best_t$specificity, 3)),
           hjust = 0, color = "red", fill = "white",
           linewidth = 0.3, size = 3.5) +
  
  annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.15,
           label = paste("AUC =", round(auc(roc_obj), 3)),
           size = 4.5, color = "steelblue", fontface = "bold") +
  
  labs(title    = paste0("ROC Curve - ",model),
       subtitle = "Red dashed lines mark the Youden's J Optimal Threshold",
       x        = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
       y        = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40"))
} 
```

## Modeling

#### Logistic Regression Models

```{r}
#fit a logistic regression model with cross validation

logistic_model <- train(
  blueWins ~ .,
  data = train,
  method = 'glm',
  family = 'binomial',
  trControl = trainControl(
    method = 'cv',
    number = 5
  )
)

summary(logistic_model) # we see a lot of NA's, likely due to multicollinearity. Will have to do variable selection. 

#make predictions
logprobs <- predict(logistic_model, newdata = x_validate, type = "prob")[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, logprobs, 'Full Logistic') #auc of 0.815

#use youden's j to make predictions
logpreds <- ifelse(logprobs > 0.483, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(logpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7435 



#do stepwise variable selection, AIC and BIC

null_model <- glm(blueWins ~ 1, data = train, family = binomial)
full_model <- glm(blueWins ~ ., data = train, family = binomial)

step_modelAIC <- step(null_model,
                   scope     = list(lower = null_model,
                                    upper = full_model),
                   direction = "both",
                   trace     = 1)

summary(step_modelAIC) # we see that it dropped down to 7 variables - not all significant though

#make predictions
aicprobs <- predict(step_modelAIC, newdata = x_validate, type = 'response')

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, aicprobs, 'Stepwise AIC') #AUC of 0.814

#use youden's j to make predictions
aicpreds <- ifelse(aicprobs > 0.497, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(aicpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7452 
#now do BIC
step_modelBIC <- step(null_model,
                   scope     = list(lower = null_model,
                                    upper = full_model),
                   direction = "both",
                   trace     = 1,
                   k = log(nrow(train)))

summary(step_modelBIC) #dropped to only three significant predictors - goldshare, bluedragons and blueexperiencediff

#make predictions
bicprobs <- predict(step_modelBIC, newdata = x_validate, type = 'response')

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, bicprobs, 'Stepwise BIC') #AUC of 0.814

#use youden's j to make predictions
bicpreds <- ifelse(bicprobs > 0.511, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(bicpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7458




#next, we will try elastic net regularization, tuning alpha first

#tune alpha for best AUC
alphas <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.05)

cv_results <- lapply(alphas, function(a) {
  cv.glmnet(x         = model.matrix(blueWins ~ ., data = train),   # must be a matrix
            y         = y_train,
            alpha     = a,
            nfolds    = 5,
            family    = "binomial", 
            type.measure = "auc")
})

best_aucs <- sapply(cv_results, function(cv) max(cv$cvm))
#best alpha
alphas[which.max(best_aucs)] #best is 0.45

#now we tune for best lambda
cv_enet <- cv.glmnet(
  x            = model.matrix(blueWins ~ ., data = train),
  y            = y_train,
  family       = "binomial",
  alpha        = 0.45,
  nfolds       = 5,
  type.measure = "auc"
)

plot(cv_enet)
cv_enet$lambda.min

#train the model
enet_model <- glmnet(
  x     = model.matrix(blueWins ~ ., data = train),
  y     = y_train,
  family = "binomial",
  alpha  = .45,
  lambda = cv_enet$lambda.min
)

coef(enet_model) #examine coefficients, many dropped to almost 0 or completely to 0. We see that all the gold related variables as well as dragons and xp share remain significant.

#make predictions
enetprobs <- predict(enet_model, newx = model.matrix(blueWins ~ ., data = validate), type = 'response')

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, enetprobs, 'Elastic Net Regularization') #AUC of 0.815

#use youden's j to make predictions
enetpreds <- ifelse(enetprobs > 0.536, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(enetpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7447


#lastly, we fit a logistic regression model on PCA transformed data

#manually tune for pca threshold
thresholds <- seq(.8, 1, by = 0.02)
results <- lapply(thresholds, function(t) {
    train(
    x         = x_train,
    y         = y_train,
    method    = "glm",
    family    = "binomial",
    preProcess = "pca",
    trControl = trainControl(
      method          = "cv",
      number          = 5,
      preProcOptions  = list(thresh = t)  
    )
  )
})

accs <- sapply(results, function(x) x$results$Accuracy)

#best threshold
thresholds[which.max(accs)] #1

#train the model
pcalogistic <- train(
  x         = x_train,
  y         = y_train,
  method    = "glm",
  family    = "binomial",
  preProcess = "pca", 
  trControl = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5,
    preProcOptions  = list(thresh = 1)  
  )
)

#make predictions
pcaprobs <- predict(pcalogistic, newdata = x_validate, type = "prob")[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, pcaprobs, 'PCA Logistic') #AUC of 0.815

#use youden's j to make predictions
pcapreds <- ifelse(pcaprobs > 0.483, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(pcapreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7435
```

#### Random Forest Models

```{r}
#first check for tree number using random forest before switching to ranger
rf_check <- randomForest(
  blueWins ~ ., 
  data = train, 
  ntree = 1000, 
  mtry = 5, 
  importance = TRUE
)

plot(rf_check,
     main = "OOB Error vs ntree")
legend("topright",
       legend = colnames(rf_check$err.rate),
       col    = 1:3,
       lty    = 1:3)
#300 trees seems to be enough, more doesn't improve much afterwards

#include tuning for mtry and node size
tune_grid <- expand.grid(
  mtry          = c(2,5,8,11),
  splitrule     = c("gini"),
  min.node.size = c(1, 5, 10)
)

rf_model <- train(
  x         = x_train,
  y         = factor(y_train,levels = c(0,1), labels = c('Red','Blue')),
  method    = "ranger",
  tuneGrid  = tune_grid,
  trControl = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5,
    classProbs      = TRUE),
  num.trees      = 300,
  importance     = "permutation"
)

#make predictions
forestprobs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, forestprobs, 'Random Forest') #AUC of 0.806

#use youden's j to make predictions
forestpreds <- ifelse(forestprobs > 0.476, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(forestpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7334
```

#### LDA and QDA Models

```{r}
#set the control
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5)

#make an lda model
lda <- train(blueWins ~ ., data = train, method = "lda", trControl = ctrl) #experience some multicollinearity problems

#make predictions
ldaprobs <- predict(lda, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, ldaprobs, 'LDA') #AUC of 0.815

#use youden's j to make predictions
ldapreds <- ifelse(ldaprobs > 0.515, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(ldapreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7424


#lets try pca with lda to tackle the multicollinearity problems
pcalda <- train(
  x         = x_train,
  y         = y_train,
  method    = "lda",
  preProcess = "pca",          # caret handles PCA internally
  trControl = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5,
    preProcOptions  = list(thresh = .8) #using an arbitrary threshold 
  )
)

#make predictions
pcaldaprobs <- predict(pcalda, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, pcaldaprobs, 'PCA LDA') #AUC of 0.814

#use youden's j to make predictions
pcaldapreds <- ifelse(pcaldaprobs > 0.51, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(pcaldapreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7424




# qda model - cant do with multicollinearity so straight to pca
pcaqda <- train(
  x         = x_train,
  y         = y_train,
  method    = "qda",
  preProcess = "pca",          # caret handles PCA internally
  trControl = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5,
    preProcOptions  = list(thresh = .8) #using an arbitrary threshold 
  )
)

#make predictions
pcaqdaprobs <- predict(pcaqda, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, pcaqdaprobs, 'PCA QDA') #AUC of 0.796

#use youden's j to make predictions
pcaqdapreds <- ifelse(pcaqdaprobs > 0.603, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(pcaqdapreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.725 




#lets try lda with our BIC reduced dataset - since they were all significant

ldabic <- train(blueWins ~ goldshare + blueDragons + blueExperienceDiff, data = train, method = "lda", trControl = ctrl)

#make predictions
ldabicprobs <- predict(ldabic, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, ldabicprobs, 'LDA Reduced') #AUC of 0.814

#use youden's j to make predictions
ldabicpreds <- ifelse(ldabicprobs > 0.516, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(ldabicpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7447



#qda on our reduced dataset

qdabic <- train(blueWins ~ goldshare + blueDragons + blueExperienceDiff, data = train, method = "qda", trControl = ctrl)

#make predictions
qdabicprobs <- predict(qdabic, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, qdabicprobs, 'QDA Reduced') #AUC of 0.813

#use youden's j to make predictions
qdabicpreds <- ifelse(qdabicprobs > 0.54, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(qdabicpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7424
```

#### KNN Model

```{r}
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5)

knn <- train(blueWins ~ ., data = train, method = "knn", tuneLength = 10, trControl = ctrl)

#make predictions
knnprobs <- predict(knn, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, knnprobs, 'KNN') #AUC of 0.785

#use youden's j to make predictions
knnpreds <- ifelse(knnprobs > 0.558, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(knnpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1') # Accuracy : 0.7103
```

#### SVM Models

```{r}
#fit a linear SVM model
svc_linear <- train(
  x          = x_train,
  y          = y_train,
  method     = "svmLinear",
  tuneLength = 10,
  trControl  = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5
  ),
  prob.model = TRUE
)

#make predictions
linearprobs <- predict(svc_linear, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, linearprobs, 'SVC - Linear') #AUC of 0.815

#use youden's j to make predictions
linearpreds <- ifelse(linearprobs > 0.523, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(linearpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7424



#try radial
svc_radial <- train(
  x          = x_train,
  y          = y_train,
  method     = "svmRadial",
  tuneLength = 10,
  trControl  = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5
  ),
  prob.model = TRUE
)

#make predictions
radialprobs <- predict(svc_radial, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, radialprobs, 'SVC - Radial') #AUC of 0.801

#use youden's j to make predictions
radialpreds <- ifelse(radialprobs > 0.627, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(radialpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7334



svc_linearbic <- train(
  blueWins ~ goldshare + blueDragons + blueExperienceDiff,
  data       = train,
  method     = "svmLinear",
  tuneLength = 10,
  trControl  = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5
  ),
  prob.model = TRUE
)

#make predictions
linearbicprobs <- predict(svc_linearbic, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, linearbicprobs, 'SVC - Linear BIC') #AUC of 0.814

#use youden's j to make predictions
linearbicpreds <- ifelse(linearbicprobs > 0.516, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(linearbicpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7447
```

#### Naive Bayes Models

```{r}
#make a naive bayes model
naivebayes <- train(
  x         = x_train,
  y         = y_train,
  method    = "naive_bayes",
  trControl = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5  
  )
)

#make predictions
naiveprobs <- predict(naivebayes, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, naiveprobs, 'Naive Bayes') #AUC of 0.812

#use youden's j to make predictions
naivepreds <- ifelse(naiveprobs > 0.33, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(naivepreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7424


#naive bayes with PCA
naivepca <- train(
  x         = x_train,
  y         = y_train,
  method    = "naive_bayes",
  preProcess = "pca",
  trControl = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5,
    preProcOptions  = list(thresh = 0.8)  
  )
)

#make predictions
naivepcaprobs <- predict(naivepca, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, naivepcaprobs, 'Naive Bayes PCA') #AUC of 0.81

#use youden's j to make predictions
naivepcapreds <- ifelse(naivepcaprobs > 0.489, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(naivepcapreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7435



#naive bayes on reduced model
naivebic <- train(
  blueWins ~ goldshare + blueDragons + blueExperienceDiff,
  data       = train,
  method     = "naive_bayes",
  trControl  = trainControl(
    method          = "cv",
    number          = 5
  )
)

#make predictions
naivebicprobs <- predict(naivebic, newdata = x_validate, type = 'prob')[,2]

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, naivebicprobs, 'Naive Bayes Reduced') #AUC of 0.814

#use youden's j to make predictions
naivebicpreds <- ifelse(naivebicprobs > 0.504, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(naivebicpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7447
```

#### Catboost Model

```{r}
#create the data pools for the catboost model
train.pool <- catboost.load_pool(data = x_train, label = as.numeric(as.character(y_train)))
val.pool <- catboost.load_pool(data = x_validate, label = as.numeric(as.character(y_validate)))
return.pool <- catboost.load_pool(data = test)

#use bayesian optimization to determine best params

#define a function that can be used for the bayesian optimization
cat_cv_score <- function(depth, learning_rate, l2_leaf_reg,
                               bagging_temperature, random_strength,
                               min_data_in_leaf, rsm) {
  train.pool <- catboost.load_pool(data = x_train, label = as.numeric(as.character(y_train))) #define inside function for parallelization
  
  params <- list(
    loss_function     = "Logloss",
    eval_metric       = "Logloss",
    depth             = as.integer(depth),
    learning_rate     = learning_rate,
    l2_leaf_reg       = l2_leaf_reg,
    bagging_temperature = bagging_temperature,
    random_strength   = random_strength,
    min_data_in_leaf  = as.integer(min_data_in_leaf),
    iterations        = 1000,
    early_stopping_rounds = 50,
    use_best_model    = TRUE,
    rsm = rsm,
    random_seed       = 42,
    logging_level     = "Silent"
  )

  cv_result <- catboost.cv(
    pool      = train.pool,
    params    = params,
    fold_count = 5,
    shuffle   = FALSE,        # FALSE for reproducibility with fixed seed
    partition_random_seed = 42
  )

  best_logloss <- min(cv_result$test.Logloss.mean)

  list(Score = -best_logloss,
       nrounds = which.min(cv_result$test.Logloss.mean))
}


#define the bounds of the hyperparameter search
bounds <- list(
  depth             = c(2L, 10L),
  learning_rate     = c(0.01, 0.1),
  l2_leaf_reg       = c(1, 15),        # CatBoost default is 3
  bagging_temperature = c(0, 1),       # 0 = no bagging, 1 = full bayesian bootstrap
  random_strength   = c(0, 6),         # adds noise to splits to prevent overfitting
  min_data_in_leaf  = c(1L, 100L),
  rsm = c(0.3, 1)                    # Random Subspace Method (feature bagging)
)

#do in parallel
cl <- makeCluster(parallel::detectCores() - 1)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Export everything workers need
clusterExport(cl, varlist = c("x_train","y_train", "cat_cv_score", "bounds"))
clusterEvalQ(cl, {
  library(catboost)
})

set.seed(42)

catbayes <- bayesOpt(
  FUN        = cat_cv_score,
  bounds     = bounds,
  initPoints = 20,
  iters.n    = 50,
  iters.k    = parallel::detectCores() - 1, 
  parallel = TRUE,
  acq        = "ei",
  eps        = 0.001,
  verbose    = 1
)

stopCluster(cl)
registerDoSEQ()

best_params <- getBestPars(catbayes)
print(best_params) #examine the best params, we see that depth and data in leaf are almost near the minimum possible, which tells us that shallower trees are optimal and that the signal in the data is quite simple

#make the final params

final_params <- list(
  loss_function         = "Logloss",
  eval_metric           = "Logloss",
  depth                 = as.integer(best_params$depth),
  learning_rate         = best_params$learning_rate,
  l2_leaf_reg           = best_params$l2_leaf_reg,
  bagging_temperature   = best_params$bagging_temperature,
  random_strength       = best_params$random_strength,
  min_data_in_leaf      = as.integer(best_params$min_data_in_leaf),
  iterations            = as.integer(catbayes$scoreSummary[
                            which.max(catbayes$scoreSummary$Score), "nrounds"]),
  random_seed           = 42,
  logging_level         = "Silent"
)


cb_final <- catboost.train(
  learn_pool = train.pool,
  params     = final_params,
)

#make predictions
catprobs <- catboost.predict(cb_final, val.pool, prediction_type = "Probability")

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, catprobs, 'Catboost') #AUC of 0.811

#use youden's j to make predictions
catpreds <- ifelse(catprobs > 0.497, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(catpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7469 
```

#### LightGBM Model

```{r}
dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(x_train), label = as.numeric(as.character(y_train)))

#create a function to input into bayesian optimizer
lgb_cv_score <- function(num_leaves, learning_rate,
                          min_data_in_leaf, lambda_l1) {
  
  dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(x_train), label = as.numeric(as.character(y_train)))
  params <- list(
    objective        = "binary",
    metric           = "binary_logloss",
    num_leaves       = as.integer(num_leaves),
    learning_rate    = learning_rate,
    max_depth        = -1, # max_depth can be -1 for no limit, or set to an integer
    min_data_in_leaf = as.integer(min_data_in_leaf),
    bagging_freq     = 5,
    bagging_fraction = 0.8,
    lambda_l1        = lambda_l1,
    feature_pre_filter = FALSE,
    verbose          = -1
  )

  cv <- lgb.cv(
    params    = params,
    data      = dtrain,
    nrounds   = 1000,
    nfold     = 5,
    early_stopping_rounds = 20,
    verbose   = -1
  )

  best_score <- -min(unlist(cv$record_evals$valid$binary_logloss$eval))
  best_iter  <- cv$best_iter

  list(Score = best_score, nrounds = best_iter)
}


#define bounds
bounds <- list(
  num_leaves       = c(2L, 30L),
  learning_rate    = c(0.01, 0.1),
  min_data_in_leaf = c(1L, round(nrow(train) * 0.01)),
  lambda_l1        = c(0, 10)
)

set.seed(42)

cl <- makeCluster(parallel::detectCores() - 1)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Export everything workers need
clusterExport(cl, varlist = c("x_train","y_train", "lgb_cv_score", "bounds"))
clusterEvalQ(cl, {
  library(lightgbm)
})

lightbayes <- bayesOpt(
  FUN        = lgb_cv_score,
  bounds     = bounds,
  initPoints = 20,     
  iters.n    = 50,     
  iters.k    = parallel::detectCores() - 1,      
  parallel   = TRUE,
  acq        = "ei",
  eps        = 0.001,
  verbose    = 1
)

stopCluster(cl)
registerDoSEQ()

lightparams <- getBestPars(lightbayes)
print(lightparams) 

final_params <- list(
  objective        = "binary",
  metric           = "binary_logloss",
  num_leaves       = as.integer(lightparams$num_leaves),
  learning_rate    = lightparams$learning_rate,
  max_depth        = -1,
  min_data_in_leaf = as.integer(lightparams$min_data_in_leaf),
  bagging_freq     = 5,
  bagging_fraction = 0.8,
  lambda_l1        = lightparams$lambda_l1,
  verbose          = 1
)

best_nrounds <- lightbayes$scoreSummary[
  which.max(lightbayes$scoreSummary$Score), "nrounds"
]

final_model <- lgb.train(
  params  = final_params,
  data    = dtrain,
  nrounds = as.integer(best_nrounds)
)

#make predictions
lightprobs <- predict(final_model, as.matrix(x_validate))

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, lightprobs, 'LightGBM') #AUC of 0.812

#use youden's j to make predictions
lightpreds <- ifelse(lightprobs > 0.513, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(lightpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.7407
```

## Neural Net Models

python library import

```{python}
import torch
from torch import nn 
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import helper_functions
from helper_functions import plot_decision_boundary, plot_decisions, accuracy_fn, print_train_time
from timeit import default_timer as timer

device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'

print(torch.__version__)
```

import data

```{python}
#can just pull from r
data = r.data
data = torch.tensor(data.values.astype(np.float32)).to(device)

train = torch.tensor(r.train.values.astype(np.float32)).to(device)
x_train = train[:,1:]
y_train = train[:,0]


validate = torch.tensor(r.validate.values.astype(np.float32)).to(device)
x_validate = validate[:,1:]
y_validate = validate[:,0]

test = torch.tensor(r.test.values.astype(np.float32)).to(device)
x_test = test[:,1:]
y_test = test[:,0]
```

create model

```{python}
class NNModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_features, output_features, act, hidden_units = 64):
        super().__init__()
        self.layer1 = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(input_features, hidden_units),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_units),
            act,
            nn.Dropout(0.3)
        )
        self.layer2 = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden_units, hidden_units),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_units),
            act,
            nn.Dropout(0.2)
        )
        self.output = nn.Linear(hidden_units, output_features)
        
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.layer1(x)
        x = self.layer2(x)
        x = self.output(x)
        return x
      
#choose a few activation functions to compare, linear was our best so we single it out
activation_functions = {
    #'ReLU' : nn.ReLU(),
    #'Sigmoid': nn.Sigmoid(),
    #'Tanh': nn.Tanh(),
    #'LeakyReLU' : nn.LeakyReLU(),
    'Linear' : nn.Identity()
}

for name, activation_function in activation_functions.items():
  fullmodel = NNModel(input_features = (len(r.train.columns) - 1), output_features = 1, act = activation_function).to(device)
      
  #loss function
  loss_fn = nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss()
  
  #Optimizer 
  optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(params = fullmodel.parameters(), lr = 0.01)

  #training
  torch.manual_seed(42)
  torch.cuda.manual_seed(42)
  
  epochs = 400
  
  #train loop
  epoch_count = []
  loss_values = []
  test_loss_values = []
  acc_values = []
  test_acc_values = []
  
  
  for epoch in range(epochs):
    #train
    fullmodel.train()
    
    #forward pass
    y_logits = fullmodel(x_train).squeeze()
    
    y_pred = torch.round(torch.sigmoid(y_logits))
    
    #calculate loss/accuracy
    loss = loss_fn(y_logits, y_train)
    acc = accuracy_fn(y_true = y_train, y_pred = y_pred)
    
    #zero grad
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    
    #back loss
    loss.backward()
    
    #step
    optimizer.step()
    
    ## testing
    fullmodel.eval()
    
    with torch.inference_mode():
      test_logits = fullmodel(x_validate).squeeze()
      test_pred = torch.round(torch.sigmoid(test_logits))
      
    #calculate loss and acc
    test_loss = loss_fn(test_logits, y_validate)
    test_acc = accuracy_fn(y_true = y_validate, y_pred = test_pred)
    
    epoch_count.append(epoch)
    loss_values.append(loss.item())
    test_loss_values.append(test_loss.item())
    acc_values.append(acc)
    test_acc_values.append(test_acc)
  
  
  #visualize
  
  
  #loss and acc
  plt.close()
  plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7))
  plt.subplot(1,2,1)
  plt.plot(epoch_count, loss_values, label="Train Loss")
  plt.plot(epoch_count, test_loss_values, label="Test Loss")
  plt.title(f"Loss Curves - {name}")
  plt.xlabel("Epochs")
  plt.ylabel("Loss")
  plt.legend(labels = [f'Train Loss:{loss.item():.4f}', f'Test Loss:{test_loss.item():.4f}'])
  #plt.ylim(0,0.1)
  plt.subplot(1,2,2)
  plt.plot(epoch_count, acc_values, label="Train Acc")
  plt.plot(epoch_count, test_acc_values, label="Test Acc")
  plt.title(f"Acc Curves - {name}")
  plt.xlabel("Epochs")
  plt.ylabel("Acc")
  plt.legend(labels = [f'Train Acc:{acc:.4f}', f'Test Acc:{test_acc:.4f}'])
  plt.show()
  
  
  
  
#make our predictions
fullmodel.eval()
  
with torch.inference_mode():
    test_logits = fullmodel(x_validate).squeeze()
    test_probs = torch.sigmoid(test_logits)
    
test_probs = test_probs.cpu().numpy()
```

bring back to r

```{r}
nnprobs <- py$test_probs

#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(validate$blueWins, nnprobs, 'Neural Net') #AUC of 0.813

#use youden's j to make predictions
nnpreds <- ifelse(nnprobs > 0.519, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(nnpreds), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')# Accuracy : 0.743
```

## Variable importance

```{r}
#shap for ranger
pfun <- function(model, newdata) {
  predict(model, newdata)$predictions[, 2]
}

ksforest <- kernelshap(
  rf_model$finalModel,
  X        = as.data.frame(x_validate[1:300, ]), #keep to 300 for speed
  bg_X     = as.data.frame(x_train[sample(nrow(x_train), 100), ]),
  pred_fun = pfun
)
shpforest <- shapviz(ksforest)
sv_importance(shpforest, kind = "beeswarm")
sv_importance(shpforest, kind = "bar")
sv_waterfall(shpforest, row_id = 1)
sv_dependence(shpforest, v = 'blueGoldDiff', color_var = "blueExperienceDiff")

#look at random forest importance values
imp <- varImp(rf_model, scale = FALSE)

plot(imp,top = 20)




#shap for logistic
pfun <- function(model, newdata) {
  predict(model, newdata)
}

kslog <- kernelshap(
  logistic_model$finalModel,
  X        = as.data.frame(x_validate[1:300, ]), #keep to 300 for speed
  bg_X     = as.data.frame(x_train[sample(nrow(x_train), 100), ]),
  pred_fun = pfun
)
shplog <- shapviz(kslog)
sv_importance(shplog, kind = "beeswarm")
sv_importance(shplog, kind = "bar")

step_modelAIC$formula
step_modelBIC$formula


#shap for catboost
shap_values <- catboost.get_feature_importance(
  cb_final,
  pool = val.pool,
  type = "ShapValues"
)
#drop the last baseline column
shap_matrix <- shap_values[, -ncol(shap_values)]
colnames(shap_matrix) <- colnames(x_validate)

shpcat <- shapviz(
  object = shap_matrix,
  X = x_validate
)

sv_importance(shpcat, kind = "beeswarm")
sv_importance(shpcat, kind = "bar")

#check regular feature importance

catboost.get_feature_importance(
  cb_final,
  pool = val.pool,
  type = "FeatureImportance"
) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
  rename(importance = "V1") %>%
  arrange(desc(importance)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(feature, importance), y = importance, fill = importance)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "steelblue", high = "darkblue") +
  labs(
    title    = "CatBoost Feature Importance",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Importance Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## Model Selection

```{r}
preds <- cbind('Full Logistic' = logpreds, 'Stepwise AIC' = aicpreds, 'Stepwise BIC' = bicpreds,'Elastic Net' = enetpreds, 'PCA Logistic' = pcapreds, 'Random Forest' = forestpreds, 'LDA' = ldapreds, 'PCA LDA' = pcaldapreds, 'PCA QDA' = pcaqdapreds, 'LDA Reduced' = ldabicpreds, 'QDA Reduced' = qdabicpreds, 'KNN' = knnpreds, 'SVC Linear' = linearpreds, 'SVC Radial' = radialpreds, 'SVC Linear Reduced' = linearbicpreds, 'Naive Bayes' = naivepreds, 'Naive Bayes PCA' = naivepcapreds, 'Naive Bayes Reduced' = naivebicpreds, 'CatBoost' = catpreds, 'LightGBM' = lightpreds, 'Neural Net' = nnpreds)

results <- sapply(1:ncol(preds), function(i) confusionMatrix(as.factor(preds[,i]), as.factor(validate$blueWins), positive = '1')$overall['Accuracy'])

#graph
data.frame(
  model    = colnames(preds),
  accuracy = as.numeric(results)
) %>%
  arrange(accuracy) %>%
  mutate(
    model    = factor(model, levels = model),
    is_best  = accuracy == max(accuracy)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = accuracy, y = model, fill = is_best)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(accuracy, accuracy = 0.1)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3, color = "grey30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#1D9E75", "FALSE" = "#378ADD")) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(as.numeric(results)) + 0.07),
    labels = scales::percent_format()
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Model accuracy comparison",
    subtitle = "Dashed line = 0.5 baseline",
    x        = "Accuracy",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.title         = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey50")
  )

```

## Model Selection

```{r}
#final predictions using our hero model, stepwise BIC

finalprobs <- predict(step_modelBIC, newdata = x_test, type = 'response')
#roc-auc analysis
roc_auc(test$blueWins, finalprobs, 'Stepwise BIC') 

#use youden's j to make predictions
finalpreds <- ifelse(finalprobs > 0.55, 1, 0)
#make a confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(finalpreds), as.factor(test$blueWins), positive = '1') 

#AUC of 0.827
# Accuracy : 0.7487

#calibration plot
calibration_plot <- function(actual, probs, model, bins = 10) {
  df <- data.frame(actual = as.numeric(as.character(actual)), 
                   probs = probs)
  
  df$bin <- cut(probs, breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 1/bins), 
                include.lowest = TRUE)
  
  df %>%
    group_by(bin) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_pred   = mean(probs),
      mean_actual = mean(actual),
      n           = n()
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = mean_pred, y = mean_actual)) +
    geom_point(aes(size = n), color = "steelblue") +
    geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
    geom_abline(lty = 2, color = "gray50") +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "n games") +
    labs(
      title    = paste("Calibration Plot —", model),
      subtitle = "Dashed line = perfect calibration",
      x        = "Mean predicted probability",
      y        = "Observed win rate"
    ) +
    xlim(0, 1) + ylim(0, 1) +
    theme_minimal()
}

calibration_plot(validate$blueWins, bicprobs, "Reduced Logistic BIC")

#shap plot
pfun <- function(model, newdata) {
  predict(model, newdata)
}

ksbic <- kernelshap(
  step_modelBIC,
  X        = as.data.frame(x_validate[1:300,c(8,16,28)]), #keep to 300 for speed
  bg_X     = as.data.frame(x_train[sample(nrow(x_train), 100),c(8,16,28)]),
  pred_fun = pfun
)
shpbic <- shapviz(ksbic)
sv_importance(shpbic, kind = "beeswarm")
sv_importance(shpbic, kind = "bar")
```
